HOOK · Why the Particle Model?
为什么用粒子模型
HOOK
•
Drop one drop of food colour into water.
滴一滴食用色素到水里。
•
Drop another into thick sugar syrup.
再滴一滴到浓 sugar syrup(糖浆)里。
•
In the water it spreads fast; in the syrup it spreads slowly.
在水里它散得快;在糖浆里它散得慢。
•
You cannot see the particles — but the spreading is evidence they are there and moving.
你看不见 particle(粒子)— 但散开就是证据,说明它们在那里,还在动。
•
Scientists who design medicines use models of these tiny particles to picture what they cannot see.
设计药物的科学家就用这些小 particle 的 model(模型)来想象看不见的东西。
•
If hot water is used, wear heat-proof gloves. Donot touch hot items with bare hands.如果用热水,要戴隔热手套。
•
不要用手直接碰烫的东西。
•
Stir sugar into any warm drink. It vanishes — but it is still there.把糖搅进任何一杯温的饮料。
•
它消失了 — 但它还在。
particlemodeltheoryevidencescientific model
LEARN · Why the Particle Model?
为什么用粒子模型
LEARN
•
A particle model uses simple shapes, like circles, to stand for particles we cannot see.
粒子 model 用简单的形状,比如圆圈,来代表我们看不见的 particle。
•
The model is not the real thing — it is a picture that helps us think and explain.
这个 model 不是真东西 — 它是一张帮我们思考和解释的图。
•
We use it because particles are far too small to see.
我们用它,是因为 particle 太小,根本看不见。
•
This model is the explaining tool for the rest of SciSpark. Every laterlesson uses it.这个 model 是 SciSpark 后面所有课的解释工具,每节课都会用到。
•
A model is useful because it is simple and shows what we cannot see.
model 有用,因为它简单,还能显示我们看不见的东西。
•
But a model is never perfect.
但 model 从来不是完美的。
•
A circle model may show where particles are, but it cannot show them moving, and the circles are not the real shape or size.
圆圈 model 能显示 particle 在哪里,但它显示不出它们在动,圆圈也不是 particle 真正的形状或大小。
•
A good model is simple AND helps us understand.
好的 model 是:既简单,又能帮我们理解。
•
Mistake: "The model is exactly what particles really look like." Wrong.
错误想法:"model 就是 particle 真正长的样子。
•
A model is a simplified picture, not a photograph.
"错。
•
Real particles are not neat circles, and a still picture cannot show them moving.
model 是一张简化的图,不是照片。
•
A model helps us think — it is not the real thing.
真正的 particle 不是整齐的圆圈,一张静止的图也显示不出它们在动。
•
model 帮我们思考 — 它不是真东西。
TRY · Why the Particle Model?
为什么用粒子模型
TRY
Q01EXPLAIN1 mark
Particles are far too small to see, even with a microscope. Explain why scientists use a model to study particles.
Particle(粒子)太小,连显微镜都看不到。 解释科学家为什么用 model 来研究 particle。
🎯 Give a reason. Use the word "because". 给一个原因。用"因为"。
Expected answer · 标准答案
Scientists use a model because particles are too small to see, so a model helps us picture and understand them.
科学家用模型，因为粒子太小看不到，模型能帮我们想象和理解它们。
Marking · 给分标准
1 mark: gives a reason linked to too small to see or easier to understand. / 1分：原因要讲到太小看不到或帮助理解。
Accept · 可收：
  • because particles are too small to see
  • models help us picture particles
  • models help us understand particles
  • because we cannot see particles directly
  • 因为粒子太小看不到
  • 模型帮助理解粒子
  • 模型帮助想象粒子
Q02STATE1 mark
A sugar cube is placed in still water and left without stirring. After some time the sugar has spread all through the water on its own. What does this tell you about the particles?
一块方糖放进静止的水里,不去搅它。 过了一段时间,糖自己散到整杯水里。 这告诉你 particle 怎么样?
🎯 One short sentence. No reasons needed. 一句短答案。不用给原因。
Expected answer · 标准答案
Particles are moving and spreading out by themselves.
粒子会自己运动并散开。
Marking · 给分标准
1 mark: states that particles move / spread out. / 1分：写粒子会运动或散开。
Accept · 可收：
  • particles move
  • particles are moving
  • particles spread out
  • particles move randomly
  • 粒子会运动
  • 粒子会自己散开
  • 粒子随机运动
TEST · Why the Particle Model?
为什么用粒子模型
EXAM MODE · NO HINTS · 考试模式
Q03GIVE1 mark
A good scientific model is simple and helps us understand. Give one reason why a model that is too complicated is not helpful.
一个好的 scientific model 是简单的,而且能帮我们理解。 给一个原因:为什么太复杂的 model 反而没用。
Expected answer · 标准答案
It is hard to understand or use because there is too much detail.
太复杂会太难理解或使用，因为细节太多。
Marking · 给分标准
1 mark: gives one clear reason why complexity makes the model less useful. / 1分：说明复杂会难理解、难使用或看不出重点。
Accept · 可收：
  • it is hard to understand
  • too much detail
  • hard to use
  • it hides the main idea
  • 太难理解
  • 细节太多
  • 不容易使用
  • 看不出重点
Q04SUGGEST2 marks
Jamila uses a model to represent the particles in a mixture of liquids. Look at the model. Suggest one strength and one limitation of this model. strength ................................................. limitation .................................................
Jamila 用一个 model 来代表一种液体混合物里的 particle。 看这个 model。 说出这个 model 的一个优点(strength)和一个限制(limitation)。 优点 ................................................. 限制 .................................................
Expected answer · 标准答案
Strength: it shows two different types of particles mixed together. Limitation: it does not show the real 3D particles or their movement.
优点：它显示两种不同粒子混在一起。限制：它没有显示真实三维粒子，也没有显示粒子运动。
Marking · 给分标准
2 marks: 1 mark for a valid strength, 1 mark for a valid limitation. / 2分：一个合理优点1分，一个合理限制1分。
Accept · 可收：
  • strength: shows two types of particles
  • strength: shows particles mixed together
  • limitation: real particles are not flat circles
  • limitation: does not show movement
  • limitation: not 3D
  • 优点：显示两种粒子
  • 优点：显示粒子混合
  • 限制：不是三维
  • 限制：没有显示运动
  • 限制：真实粒子不是平面圆圈
Q05EXPLAIN1 mark
A student tests how fast colour spreads in water. She wants to compare warm water and cold water in a fair test. Explain why she should use the same amount of colour in each glass.
一个学生测颜色在水里散开有多快。她想公平地比较温水和冷水(fair test)。 解释为什么她每杯应该用一样多的颜色。
Expected answer · 标准答案
So the amount of colour does not affect the result; only the water temperature is changed.
这样颜色的量不会影响结果；只改变水温。
Marking · 给分标准
1 mark: links same amount to a fair test / only temperature changes. / 1分：讲到公平测试或只改变水温。
Accept · 可收：
  • so it is a fair test
  • only temperature changes
  • same amount makes the comparison fair
  • colour amount will not affect the result
  • 公平测试
  • 只改变水温
  • 颜色的量不影响结果
  • 控制变量
Q06STATE1 mark
A scientist draws particles as small round balls. STATE whether the particles in real matter are exactly like small round balls, or whether the balls are just a model.
一个科学家把 particle 画成小圆球。 真实 matter 里的 particle 是不是真的就像小圆球,还是说这些球 只是一个 model?
Expected answer · 标准答案
The balls are just a model; real particles are not exactly small round balls.
这些小球只是模型；真实粒子不完全是小圆球。
Marking · 给分标准
1 mark: states that the balls are a model, not the exact real particles. / 1分：说明小球只是模型，不是真实完全一样。
Accept · 可收：
  • they are just a model
  • balls are a model
  • real particles are not exactly balls
  • not exactly small round balls
  • 只是模型
  • 不是真实样子
  • 真实粒子不完全是小圆球
Q07GIVE1 mark
Scientists keep a model only while it agrees with what they observe. GIVE the word for the facts and observations that support a model.
科学家只在 model 跟他们观察到的一致时才保留它。 支持一个 model 的事实和观察,用哪个词来称呼?
Expected answer · 标准答案
Evidence.
证据。
Marking · 给分标准
1 mark: evidence / observations / data. / 1分：写证据、观察或数据。
Accept · 可收：
  • evidence
  • observations
  • data
  • 证据
  • 观察证据
  • 数据
Q08EXPLAIN1 mark
A smell from cooking slowly spreads across a whole room, even when the air is still. Explain how the particle model helps us picture why the smell spreads.
做饭的气味慢慢散满整个房间,就算空气没流动也一样。 解释:粒子 model 怎么帮我们想象气味为什么会散开?
Expected answer · 标准答案
The smell is made of tiny particles that move and spread through the air.
气味由很小的粒子组成，这些粒子会在空气中运动并散开。
Marking · 给分标准
1 mark: explains the smell spreading using moving particles. / 1分：用粒子运动解释气味扩散。
Accept · 可收：
  • smell particles move through air
  • particles spread out through the air
  • tiny particles move randomly
  • the smell spreads because particles move
  • 气味粒子会运动
  • 粒子在空气中散开
  • 粒子随机运动
Q09IDENTIFY1 mark
Three students describe what a scientific model is: A: a model is the exact real thing, copied perfectly. B: a model is a simple idea that helps us picture something. C: a model is always wrong and never useful. IDENTIFY which student is correct.
三个学生描述什么是 scientific model(科学模型): A:model 是完全照搬的真东西。 B:model 是一个简单的想法,帮我们想象某样东西。 C:model 永远是错的,从来没用。 哪个学生说对了?
Expected answer · 标准答案
Student B.
学生 B。
Marking · 给分标准
1 mark: identifies B. / 1分：选B。
Accept · 可收：
  • B
  • student B
  • B is correct
  • 学生B
  • B同学
Q10SUGGEST2 marks
A teacher uses round wooden balls in a tray to model the particles in a solid. Suggest one strength and one limitation of using wooden balls as a model of particles. strength ................................................. limitation .................................................
一位老师用盘子里的圆木球来 model 一个固体里的 particle。 说出用木球当 particle model 的一个优点(strength)和一个限制 (limitation)。 优点 ................................................. 限制 .................................................
Expected answer · 标准答案
Strength: the wooden balls show particles packed closely in a solid. Limitation: real particles are not wooden balls and are much smaller.
优点：木球显示固体粒子紧密排列。限制：真实粒子不是木球，而且小得多。
Marking · 给分标准
2 marks: 1 mark for a valid strength, 1 mark for a valid limitation. / 2分：一个合理优点1分，一个合理限制1分。
Accept · 可收：
  • strength: shows particles packed closely
  • strength: shows particles in a solid are close together
  • limitation: real particles are much smaller
  • limitation: particles are not wooden balls
  • limitation: does not show vibration
  • 优点：显示紧密排列
  • 优点：显示固体粒子靠得很近
  • 限制：真实粒子小得多
  • 限制：粒子不是木球
  • 限制：没有显示振动
WRAP · Why the Particle Model?
为什么用粒子模型
WRAP
📝 SUMMARY · 总结
•
A particle model is a simple thinking tool that lets us picture particles we cannot see.
粒子 model 是一个简单的思考工具,让我们能想象看不见的 particle。
•
It has strengths (simple, shows the unseen) and limits (cannot show everything).
它有优点(简单、显示看不见的东西),也有限制(显示不了所有东西)。
•
It is not the real thing.
它不是真东西。
•
Particles turn out to be made of just 90+ basic building blocks — Atoms!
原来 particle 是由 90 多种基本的东西组成的 — Atoms(原子)!
•
Next lesson: atoms and elements.
下一课:原子和元素。
🧠 MEMORY HOOK · 口诀金句
Model = map，不是真世界本身.
记忆法：Model = map，不是真世界本身。
🔁 COVER & RECALL · 盖住回想
A teacher uses round wooden balls in a tray to model the particles in a solid. Suggest one strength and one limitation of using wooden balls as a model of parti
一位老师用盘子里的圆木球来 model 一个固体里的 particle。 说出用木球当 particle model 的一个优点(strength)和一个限制 (limitation)。 优点 .......................
Strength: the wooden balls show particles packed closely in a solid. Limitation: real particles are not wooden balls and are much smaller.
优点：木球显示固体粒子紧密排列。限制：真实粒子不是木球，而且小得多。
Hi! Ready to learn?
嗨!准备好了吗?
